누가복음 20:1.content: On one of those days, as he was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the Good News, the priests and scribes came to him with the elders.

누가복음 20:2.content: They asked him, “Tell us: by what authority do you do these things? Or who is giving you this authority?”

누가복음 20:3.content: He answered them, “I also will ask you one question. Tell me:

누가복음 20:4.content: the baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?”

누가복음 20:5.content: They reasoned with themselves, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’

누가복음 20:6.content: But if we say, ‘From men,’ all the people will stone us, for they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”

누가복음 20:7.content: They answered that they didn’t know where it was from.

누가복음 20:8.content: Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”

누가복음 20:9.content: He began to tell the people this parable. “A man planted a vineyard, and rented it out to some farmers, and went into another country for a long time.

누가복음 20:10.content: At the proper season, he sent a servant to the farmers to collect his share of the fruit of the vineyard. But the farmers beat him, and sent him away empty.

누가복음 20:11.content: He sent yet another servant, and they also beat him, and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.

누가복음 20:12.content: He sent yet a third, and they also wounded him, and threw him out.

누가복음 20:13.content: The lord of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son. It may be that seeing him, they will respect him.’

누가복음 20:14.content: “But when the farmers saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.’

누가복음 20:15.content: They threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do to them?

누가복음 20:16.content: He will come and destroy these farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.” When they heard that, they said, “May that never be!”

누가복음 20:17.content: But he looked at them and said, “Then what is this that is written, ‘The stone which the builders rejected was made the chief cornerstone?’

누가복음 20:18.content: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, but it will crush whomever it falls on to dust.”

누가복음 20:19.content: The chief priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour, but they feared the people—for they knew he had spoken this parable against them.

누가복음 20:20.content: They watched him and sent out spies, who pretended to be righteous, that they might trap him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the power and authority of the governor.

누가복음 20:21.content: They asked him, “Teacher, we know that you say and teach what is right, and aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God.

누가복음 20:22.content: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”

누가복음 20:23.content: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them, “Why do you test me?

누가복음 20:24.content: Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?” They answered, “Caesar’s.”

누가복음 20:25.content: He said to them, “Then give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”

누가복음 20:26.content: They weren’t able to trap him in his words before the people. They marveled at his answer and were silent.

누가복음 20:27.content: Some of the Sadducees came to him, those who deny that there is a resurrection.

누가복음 20:28.content: They asked him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies having a wife, and he is childless, his brother should take the wife and raise up children for his brother.

누가복음 20:29.content: There were therefore seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died childless.

누가복음 20:30.content: The second took her as wife, and he died childless.

누가복음 20:31.content: The third took her, and likewise the seven all left no children, and died.

누가복음 20:33.content: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them will she be? For the seven had her as a wife.”

누가복음 20:34.content: Jesus said to them, “The children of this age marry, and are given in marriage.

누가복음 20:35.content: But those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.

누가복음 20:36.content: For they can’t die any more, for they are like the angels, and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.

누가복음 20:37.content: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed at the bush, when he called the Lord ‘The God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’

누가복음 20:38.content: Now he is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all are alive to him.”

누가복음 20:39.content: Some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you speak well.”

누가복음 20:40.content: They didn’t dare to ask him any more questions.

누가복음 20:41.content: He said to them, “Why do they say that the Christ is David’s son?

누가복음 20:42.content: David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,

누가복음 20:43.content: until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.” ’

누가복음 20:44.content: “David therefore calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”

누가복음 20:45.content: In the hearing of all the people, he said to his disciples,

누가복음 20:46.content: “Beware of those scribes who like to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces, the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts;

누가복음 20:47.content: who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers: these will receive greater condemnation.”
